summary: Docker reproduction confirms command injection through very_dangerous_raw_string argument type, allowing arbitrary shell command execution via multiple injection vectors.
expected_security_impact: Running the PoC should execute arbitrary commands inside the container through shell command injection, creating proof files demonstrating successful command execution.
actual_observed_result: Executing ./reproduction/run.sh built OliveTin, started service, and successfully injected commands through multiple shell metacharacter payloads (;, $(), backticks, &&), creating proof files with command output.
verification_method: Script executes multiple command injection payloads via StartAction API endpoint and verifies creation of proof files (/tmp/cmd_injection_proof*.txt) containing arbitrary command output (id, whoami, /etc/passwd, etc.).
evidence_produced_by_run: Proof files created with command output (id, whoami, /etc/passwd)
Commands executed as OliveTin process user
Shell metacharacters not sanitized in very_dangerous_raw_string type
Direct shell execution via sh -c confirmed in logs

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones OliveTin from GitHub, creates config with very_dangerous_raw_string argument, builds and runs container
